concepts.title: Net Factor Income from Abroad (NFIA) is subtracted from National Income (NI) to get Gross National Income (GNI)
concepts.description: This is a crucial point to remember when calculating GNI. NFIA represents the income earned by a nation from abroad, which is not part of its NI.

concepts.title: The concept of net national income (NNI) is an important aspect of GNI, which takes into account NFIA and personal disposable income.
concepts.description: NNI is a key component of GNI, and students should be able to explain its significance and relationship with GNI.

concepts.title: The formula for calculating National Income (NI) is: NI = C + I + G + (X - M)
concepts.description: This formula is a fundamental concept in economics and is frequently asked in exams. Students should be able to derive it from the consumption function and investment function.

concepts.title: Use the 'CIGXM' mnemonic to remember the components of National Income: Consumption (C) + Investment (I) + Government Expenditure (G) + (Exports - Imports) (X - M)
concepts.description: This mnemonic helps students remember the components of National Income in a simple and memorable way.

concepts.title: Not distinguishing between Gross National Income (GNI) and Net National Income (NNI)
concepts.description: Students often confuse GNI and NNI. GNI is the total income earned by a nation, while NNI is the disposable income available to the citizens of a nation. Students should be able to explain the difference and calculate both correctly.

concepts.title: The formula for calculating Personal Disposable Income (PDI) is: PDI = NNI - Taxes - Subsidies
concepts.description: This formula is a key component of measuring personal disposable income. Students should be able to derive it from the concept of NNI and explain its significance.

concepts.title: The concept of National Income Accounting (NIA) is based on the Circular Flow of Income and Expenditure.
concepts.description: This concept is a fundamental aspect of understanding National Income and Related Aggregates. Students should be able to explain the circular flow and its relationship with NIA.

concepts.title: The National Income and Related Aggregates are: National Income (NI), Net National Income (NNI), Gross National Income (GNI), Personal Disposable Income (PDI), and Net Domestic Product (NDP)
concepts.description: These are the key concepts that students should be familiar with. They should be able to explain and calculate each of these concepts correctly.
